key_insight: General-purpose LLMs can follow multiple 3D spatial constraints in molecule generation, but they still trail specialized diffusion models on pocket-conditioned design.
clinical_relevance: This points to a potential new route for faster structure-based drug discovery, where models could help propose candidate binders under complex protein-pocket constraints.
method: Benchmarked general-purpose LLMs against specialized diffusion baselines for 3D ligand generation.
Tested multi-conditioned setups using protein pockets plus anchor fragments, pharmacophore points, and required pocket-ligand interactions.
Introduced 3D-Fit, a token-efficient framework for evaluating constrained spatial molecule generation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a systematic benchmark showing LLMs are promising at handling multiple spatial constraints simultaneously, though still behind state-of-the-art methods.
watch_item: Performance is promising but not yet competitive with specialized models, so real-world use will require careful validation on diverse targets and constraint types.
paper_title: Do Language Models Dream of Binding Molecules? Benchmarking LLMs under Spatial Constraints
